vitals.terrain: mountain roads, wooded climbs and descents
vitals.time_limit: 10 mph progression speed minimum; timed segments 10-23 mph

course_description.overview: SENA Gran Fondo National Series event with three routes (95, 60, 25 miles) through Blue Ridge Mountains featuring timed segments on mountain roads with climbs beside Winklers Creek, Bethel Valley, and Tennessee State Line, returning to downtown Boone.

key_segments.name: Winklers Creek Climb
key_segments.description: First timed section after Appalachian State University.

key_segments.name: Bethel Valley to Tennessee State Line
key_segments.description: Climb ending the fourth timed section.

key_segments.name: Laurel Fork and White Oak roads

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Roller-coaster mountain roads with challenging but not overly long climbs, wooded descents, and high country elevation; timed-segment racing format where only segment times count for rankings.

logistics.getting_there: Start/finish at 164 S. Depot St., Boone, NC 28607 near Appalachian Voices.
logistics.registration_details: Register via BikeReg; packet pickup Saturday 3-6 PM and Sunday 6-7:15 AM at start location; bib, bike plate, timing chip required.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike recommended; bike plate mandatory on handlebar.
logistics.local_considerations: Mid-summer high country with 80°F temps; post-race party in downtown Boone with food tickets, Appalachian Voices lounge access.

final_verdict.one_liner: Fun, scenic high-country gran fondo with timed segments and mountain challenges in cooler summer temps.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking timed KOM-style racing on rolling mountain roads without full mass-start pressure.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the series prestige, scenery, and party vibe in Boone's Blue Ridge Mountains.
pro_tips: Maintain 10+ mph progression to avoid cutoffs.
Pin bib correctly and mount bike plate for timing.
Bring dietary backups as aid is standard.
Stage by 7:45 AM sharp for 8:00 AM neutral start.
